title: Lab 47: Per-User Password Aging
description: Apply per-user password aging settings without adding extra account noise.

tasks: Create user cycle47 and set the password to cinder9.
Configure cycle47 with a maximum password age of 30 days, a minimum age of 2 days, and a warning period of 7 days.
Force cycle47 to change the password at the next login.
hints: This lab is about chage, not about home-directory behavior.
Use chage -d 0 to force a change at next login.
checks: getent passwd cycle47 >/dev/null && passwd -S cycle47 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $2}' | grep -Eq '^(P|PS)$'

chage -l cycle47 | grep -Eq '^Maximum number of days between password change[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*30$' && chage -l cycle47 | grep -Eq '^Minimum number of days between password change[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*2$' && chage -l cycle47 | grep -Eq '^Number of days of warning before password expires[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*7$'
chage -l cycle47 | grep -Eq '^Last password change[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*password must be changed$'
solution_outline: Create the user and set the password.
Apply the max, min, and warning values with chage.
task_titles: Create cycle47 and set the password
Apply the requested password aging values
Expire the password for the next login
